mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, consumer confidence and wage growth accelerate, driving aggregate demand higher and supporting corporate revenue growth, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and employment growth signals a broad-based economic expansion that improves corporate profit margins. The joint signal confirms demand resilience, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 1500 and PAYEMS rises above 145000
failure_condition: The joint HOUST and PAYEMS signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates immediately into higher corporate sales and positive equity returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO increases consistently precede negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent confirms robust economic momentum that sustains earnings revisions upward. This growth signal drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 3.0 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are flat or negative in over 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0 percent across any 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low unemployment and high total employment validates a sustainable labor market recovery. This dual confirmation reduces recession risk premiums, compressing equity risk spreads and lifting S&P 500 returns over a 60-day window.
failure_condition: The combined UNRATE and PAYEMS threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 3-year rolling period, or forward returns fail to beat the market median by 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential mortgage debt outstanding indicates expanding household leverage and housing market activity, which historically precedes positive equity market performance. This credit expansion signal generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS increases consistently precede negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations over a 10-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 2.5 percent while the federal funds rate stays below 1.0 percent, the Federal Reserve faces a policy dilemma that typically triggers market volatility and repricing of inflation expectations. This mismatch generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year increase exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
failure_condition: The joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS condition produces positive S&P 500 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered instances across any 5-year rolling window.
